channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: In a Rarity, Republicans Stand Up to Trump
item.description: President Trump faced a wall of opposition from Senate G.O.P. lawmakers, in part over his plan to create a $1.8 billion fund to reward his allies.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Senator Susan Collins, Republican of Maine, is known to oppose the most extreme parts of President Trump’s agenda.

item.title: Inside the Senate G.O.P. Meltdown Over Trump’s Fund
item.description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, went to Capitol Hill to allay Republicans’ concerns over a fund to pay people who claim government mistreatment. It did not go well.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Thune, Republican of South Dakota and the majority leader, said his colleagues had “very legitimate questions” about the new fund.

item.title: Trump Faces Rare G.O.P. Revolt, and Democrats Mock Their Own Party’s Report
item.description: Plus, the Friday news quiz.
item.dc:creator: Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Luke Broadwater and Nate Cohn
item.media:description: President Trump faced a wall of opposition from Senate G.O.P. lawmakers, in part over his plan to create a $1.8 billion fund to reward his allies.

item.title: Iran and Oman in Talks Over Strait of Hormuz Ship Payment System
item.description: The discussions suggest that the United States and the Iranian government may not be close to reaching a deal to end a war that has badly damaged the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, Vivian Nereim, Erika Solomon and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz last month.

item.title: GOP Pulls Measure on Trump’s War Powers in Iran, Lacking Votes to Defeat It
item.description: House Republican leaders abruptly scrapped a planned vote on a measure to direct President Trump to end the conflict or win authorization for it, amid party defections and absences.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro, Robert Jimison and Michael Gold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Stocks Rise, Oil Prices Wobble on Impasse Over Reopening Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Nearly three months since the fighting began, disagreements remain over the fate of Iran’s uranium stockpile and transit fees for the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Move Over, Private Equity. It’s Great to Be a Banker Again.
item.description: They played second fiddle to private equity and hedge funds for years, but 2026 is shaping up to be “the year of the bank,” one consultant said.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland

item.title: Asian Currencies Hit by Iran War, Adding to Economic Strain
item.description: Soaring oil prices and a surging dollar are testing Asia’s foreign-exchange reserves, which were built up after the 1997 crisis.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.media:description: A worker hauling frozen fish at a port in Pati, Indonesia. Rising diesel prices have left many boats docked.

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Last ‘Late Show’: Laughing Well Is the Best Revenge
item.description: The “Late Show” cancellation was a disappointment. But a surreally lovely final episode turned it into a cancellebration.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:description: Stephen Colbert and an array of famous friends gave “The Late Show” a joyous send-off on Thursday night.

item.title: In Defense of the Institution of Late Night
item.description: “The Late Show With Stephen Colbert” has ended. Our culture critic Jason Zinoman describes what we stand to lose.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman, Haimy Assefa, Gabriel Blanco and Laura Salaberry
item.category: Late Show With David Letterman (TV Program)

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Last ‘Late Show’ Features Famous Friends and Paul McCartney
item.description: Colbert was joined by famous friends, fellow hosts and special guests like Paul McCartney for the finale of “The Late Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:description: Stephen Colbert’s final “Late Show” included celebrity guests, musical performances and an absurdist final bit.

item.title: Stephen Colbert Suited Himself
item.description: The suit may be a totem of conventionality, but for late-night hosts, it has become a useful tool.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:description: Stephen Colbert during the “Late Show” finale on Thursday.

item.title: Memorial Day Travel Forecast: Gas Prices, Traffic and Flights
item.description: Most U.S. travelers will be driving to their destinations for the long weekend, kicking off what is expected to be a busy summer travel season.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:description: A record 45 million Americans are expected to hit the highways over the long weekend, according to AAA.

item.title: With Gas Prices Through the Roof, Will Renting an EV or Hybrid Car Save You Money?
item.description: With gas prices through the roof, more travelers are considering alternative vehicles to control costs. But how much will you really save?
item.dc:creator: Elaine Glusac
item.media:credit: Drew Kelly for The New York Times
item.media:description: The great American road trip is at risk this year because of rising fuel prices. Some travelers are looking to rent hybrid or electric vehicles to save money.

item.title: Memorial Day Weekend Will Be Wet and Cool For Much of the U.S.
item.description: Memorial Day weekend will be wet for much of the United States, though dry, seasonal weather is likely in the West.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar
item.media:description: Widespread showers are expected over the holiday weekend.

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooter So Alarmed Police, They Seized Father’s Guns
item.description: In 2025, Caleb Vazquez’s obsession with mass shooters and Nazism prompted reports to the authorities. His father surrendered dozens of firearms.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín, Tim Arango and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: Families escorted their children across police lines after a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

item.title: ‘They Protected You Guys’: Mourners Honor Heroes in Mosque Shooting
item.description: Thousands filled a San Diego park to recite a funeral prayer for the three men killed by gunmen on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners gathered for a funeral prayer for three men who died on Monday while confronting two shooters at the Islamic Center of San Diego.

item.title: The Civil Rights Era Is Collapsing Before Our Eyes
item.description: America is witnessing the greatest decimation of Black political power in over a century.
item.dc:creator: Nikole Hannah-Jones

item.title: Before Europe Anoints Someone to Talk to Putin, It Debates What to Talk About
item.description: Europeans are considering appointing an envoy to Ukraine peace talks with Russia. First, many warn, they need to decide what to ask.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek, Valerie Hopkins and Paul Sonne
item.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia in Moscow, this month.

item.title: From General to Governor: Putin Pushes a ‘New Elite’ of War Veterans
item.description: The popular leader of a region bordering Ukraine is replaced by a military commander as President Vladimir V. Putin looks to reward those who fought in his war.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:description: A photo released by Russian state media showed President Vladimir V. Putin meeting with Alexander Shuvayev in Moscow this month on the day Mr. Shuvayev was appointed to lead the Belgorod region.

item.title: Partisan Mud Fight, or Focus on the Midterms? Redistricting Divides Democrats.
item.description: Gov. Abigail Spanberger of Virginia urged her fellow Democrats, including the House minority leader, Hakeem Jeffries, to stop talking about drawing new congressional lines for partisan advantage.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein, Lisa Lerer and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times, Pool photo by Mike Kropf
item.media:description: Representative Hakeem Jeffries of New York and Gov. Abigail Spanberger of Virginia.

item.title: Poll Suggests a Possible Path Forward for Democrats
item.description: The divisions are clear, but there’s more common ground than one might expect within the coalition, a Times/Siena poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn
item.media:credit: Illustration by Akshita Chandra/The New York Times; Photograph by Ryasick, via Getty Images

item.title: 5 Notable Moments From Candace Owens’s Warm Interview With Hunter Biden
item.description: In a nearly two-hour conversation, Mr. Biden discussed his addiction to drugs and alcohol, and Ms. Owens apologized for her past remarks about him.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

item.title: On the Ground in South Sudan: Why Akobo Faces an Ebola Risk
item.description: Hunger and conflict are on the minds of the residents of Akobo, where an outbreak could have devastating consequences.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Ed Ram
item.media:credit: Ed Ram for The New York Times

item.title: Ebola Outbreak: What to Know About Bundibugyo Virus Symptoms and Spread
item.description: Here’s what to know about the symptoms and spread of the virus, which has sickened people in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.
item.dc:creator: Dani Blum

item.title: In West Bank, Latest Victim of Israeli Settler Violence Shocks in a New Way
item.description: A video of a Palestinian family’s dog being savagely beaten has spread widely in the days after the attack.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: A dog being beaten by a settler in the West Bank village of Atara in May.

item.title: Everlane Finalizes Sale to Shein, the Fast Fashion Giant
item.description: The ultra low priced retailer has purchased Everlane, the millennial standby that promised transparency.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Justin Kaneps for The New York Times
item.media:description: Founded in 2011, Everlane was positioned as a environmentally conscious label.

item.title: The Miles Davis Look: His Sunglasses, Trumpet, Hair and More
item.description: Fast cars, huge shades and, surprisingly, any old trumpet: These are the things that made Miles Miles.
item.dc:creator: Louis Lucero II
item.media:credit: From left; Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images; Christian Rose/Roger Viollet, via Getty Images; Mark Patiky/Condé Nast, via Getty Images; David Redfern/Redferns

item.title: Guy Savoy Awarded Légion d’Honneur
item.description: Guy Savoy, who worked his way up from modest beginnings (and a recent setback), this week became the first chef inducted into the prestigious Académie des Beaux-Arts.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Sciolino
item.media:credit: Samuel Aranda for The New York Times

item.title: StreetEasy’s ‘Reserve Your Future’ Is Taking Reservations for 2046
item.description: To celebrate 20 years in business, a New York real-estate site is offering restaurant reservations two decades in the future, an unusual offer in a city of short shelf lives.
item.dc:creator: Pete Wells
item.media:credit: Adam Friedlander for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gage & Tollner, which dates back to 1879, is one of five New York restaurants taking a handful of reservations for 2046 in a promotional campaign for StreetEasy.

item.title: A.I. Is a Job Creator
item.description: A.I. will create more jobs than it will kill.
item.dc:creator: David M. Solomon

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Finale Didn’t Mean What It Seemed To
item.description: You’re going to miss corporate media.
item.dc:creator: Michael Hirschorn

item.title: Get Ready to Grill
item.description: A note for Memorial Day.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Christopher Simpson for The New York Times

item.title: Why Islamophobic, Antisemitic and Racist Crimes Are Rising in the UK
item.description: Islamophobic, antisemitic and racist crimes are being fueled by online disinformation, global instability and divisive political rhetoric, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.media:credit: Jamie Lashmar/Press Assocation, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Outside a mosque in Peacehaven, England, after an arson attack in October.

item.title: New Taliban Decree on Divorce Formalizes Child Marriage in Afghanistan, U.N. Warns
item.description: A new Afghan law requires girls to wait until puberty before seeking to get out of a marriage. It also requires mediation for women seeking to escape an abusive husband.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pharmacy in Kabul, Afghanistan, on Tuesday. The Taliban government has imposed some of the world’s toughest restrictions on women and girls.
